fixture.description: New code calls CountDownLatch.countDown() BEFORE assigning the result it is meant to guard, so a waiter released from await() can read a null/stale result (happens-before edge placed at the wrong point). Single writer — not a lost-update race.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that `done.countDown()` is called before `result` is assigned, so a thread released from `awaitResult()` (`done.await()`) can observe `result` as null or a stale value; the fix is to write `result` before signaling completion. Acceptable severity: CRITICAL or IMPORTANT. FAIL if the publish-before-write ordering bug is not surfaced. The report should NOT claim the `volatile` modifier is the bug, nor describe it as a lost-update race (there is a single writer).

expected_behavior: Concurrency agent flags the countDown-before-assign ordering inversion
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim volatile is wrong or call it a lost-update race
